questionHtml: Explain why the gas in configuration B has a greater number of microstates than in A.
markschemeHtml:
Configuration A has only one microstate ✓ 

In configuration B, pairs of particles can be swapped between the halves ✓ 

Every such change gives rise to a new microstate «so there is a large number of microstates in B» ✓

meta.title: Explain why the gas in configuration B has a greater number of microstates than in A.
